name: Libyan Civil Wars (2011 and 2014-2020)
summary: The Libyan Civil Wars include the 2011 uprising and civil war that overthrew Muammar Gaddafi, followed by renewed civil war from 2014 to 2020 among rival governments, militias, foreign-backed coalitions, and jihadist groups. Casualty accounting is fragmented: official Libyan reassessments, ACLED/UCDP event data, UN/UNSMIL reports, HRW/Amnesty investigations, and Airwars/New America strike databases use different definitions and verification thresholds. A defensible combined range is roughly 25,000 to 37,500 deaths, with major uncertainty around missing persons, mass graves, and indirect mortality.

sides.name: Gaddafi government and loyalist forces
members: foreign fighters aligned with the regime

military_killed.notes: Approximate 2013 Libyan government reassessment: loyalist combatant deaths were said to be similar to the 4,700 rebel-side dead. The figure is incomplete and does not fully resolve missing persons or executions after capture.

breakdown.victims_group: civilians in Misrata and other rebel-held towns
breakdown.notes: HRW documented at least 16 civilians killed in indiscriminate attacks around Misrata over 14-17 April 2011; the UN Commission found sustained shelling and use of unguided munitions in residential areas.

breakdown.victims_group: civilians and protesters perceived as opposition supporters
breakdown.notes: Public summaries of 2011 casualty data report 152-168 civilians deliberately killed by pro-Gaddafi forces. This should be treated as a documented component, not a complete total.

breakdown.victims_group: captured fighters and civilians detained by government forces
breakdown.notes: Amnesty found strong evidence of deliberate killings of captured fighters, enforced disappearances and torture, but no complete numeric national total.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The total number of civilians directly killed by Gaddafi forces is unknown. Documented components include shelling, cluster munitions, protest killings, detention deaths and executions.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: civilians in areas mined or contaminated by Qadhafi forces
deaths_from_actions.notes: The UN Commission found Qadhafi forces used cluster munitions and anti-personnel/anti-vehicle mines, including in civilian areas, and did not properly record minefields. No reliable death total was found.

military_killed.notes: 2013 Libyan government reassessment reported 4,700 rebel fighters/supporters killed and 2,100 missing across both camps. The label 'supporters' may include non-combatant activists.

breakdown.victims_group: captured Gaddafi loyalists and detainees around Sirte
breakdown.notes: HRW documented apparent executions by Misrata-based militias after Gaddafi's capture; secondary summaries describe over 60 loyalists executed. Exact civilian/combatant status varies because many victims were captured fighters hors de combat.
The UN Commission found Tawerghans unlawfully killed by thuwar, predominantly from Misrata, but did not provide a complete count in the cited findings.
The UN Commission concluded that the thuwar attack on Sirte was indiscriminate because of the scale of destruction and weapons used.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: No complete civilian death total attributable to anti-Gaddafi forces was found. The numeric low reflects a documented execution component and should not be read as a full total.

military_killed.notes: No NATO military personnel were killed in combat during the 2011 Libya operation.

breakdown.notes: UN Commission documented five NATO airstrikes in which 60 civilians were killed and 55 injured.
HRW documented 72 civilian deaths in eight NATO airstrike incidents.
Airwars later estimated 223-403 likely civilian deaths from NATO strikes, using a broader open-source methodology than UN/HRW verified-site counts.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The low reflects the UN Commission verified count; the high reflects Airwars' broader likely range. HRW's 72 documented deaths sits between these.

sides.name: House of Representatives / Libyan National Army-LAAF camp
members: Libyan National Army / Libyan Arab Armed Forces

military_killed.notes: No reliable comprehensive side-specific count of LNA/LAAF combatant deaths for 2014-2020 was found. Aggregate ACLED totals for Libya in 2014-2020 are about 16,012 deaths across all sides and civilians.

breakdown.notes: New America/Airwars attributed 2,348 strikes to the LNA since 2014, causing 214-356 civilian deaths.

New America/Airwars attributed at least 131 UAE strikes in Libya to 135-185 civilian deaths; UAE strikes were generally in support of the LNA camp. These may overlap conceptually with LNA-aligned operations.

breakdown.victims_group: migrants and refugees at Tajoura detention centre
breakdown.notes: 3 July 2019 Tajoura detention centre strike; New America attributes the bombing to the UAE in support of the LNA and reports at least 53 civilians killed.
4 August 2019 Murzuq strike; reported civilian deaths were about 42-43, with local/GNA sources blaming the LNA, while the LNA denied targeting civilians.

breakdown.victims_group: Tarhuna civilians and perceived opponents of al-Kaniyat
breakdown.notes: GNA authorities reported at least 115 unidentified bodies in 26 mass grave sites in Tarhuna by mid-November 2020. These deaths are not added to the LNA strike subtotal because some may overlap with missing-person counts and the full time period extends before 2019.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The 349-541 range is the sum of LNA-attributed and UAE-attributed airstrike civilian-death ranges from New America/Airwars. This is not a complete civilian-death total for the LNA camp and may include coordination/attribution overlap.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: returning civilians and clearance personnel in southern Tripoli and surrounding areas
deaths_from_actions.notes: OCHA/Libya Herald reporting said explosive contamination after the 2020 withdrawal caused 206 casualties since May 2020, including 73 killed and 133 injured.

sides.name: Tripoli / GNA / Libya Dawn-aligned camp
members: US counter-ISIS forces operating with GNA consent

military_killed.notes: No reliable comprehensive side-specific count of GNA/Libya Dawn combatant deaths for 2014-2020 was found.

breakdown.notes: New America/Airwars attributed 453 GNA strikes to 53-72 civilian deaths.

breakdown.victims_group: Libyan civilians near ISIS targets, mainly Sirte
breakdown.notes: US strikes on ISIS targets in Libya, conducted with GNA consent, reportedly caused 11-21 civilian deaths according to New America/Airwars.

UNSMIL attributed 21 civilian deaths in airstrikes and 4 in ground fighting to GNA-affiliated forces in April-June 2020. This may overlap temporally with broader strike datasets only after February 2020.
Amnesty reported at least 5 civilians killed by a GNA artillery attack on 14 May 2019.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: The 64-93 range adds New America/Airwars GNA strike deaths and US counter-ISIS deaths; it does not include every GNA-aligned militia abuse and may overlap with UNSMIL period reports.

sides.name: Islamic State and other jihadist armed groups

military_killed.notes: ISIS and other jihadist combatants were killed by GNA/Misrata forces, LNA forces, and US strikes, especially in Sirte, but no reliable full combatant-death total was found.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: ISIS and jihadist groups committed executions, bombings and assassinations, but I did not find a complete civilian-fatality total separated by actor for 2014-2020.

totals.notes: Military_low sums the two 2011 side-specific combatant estimates only: 4,700 anti-Gaddafi plus about 4,700 loyalist dead. Civilian_low uses Airwars' 2011 all-belligerents civilian-harm minimum as a documented floor, not a full civilian total. Grand_low combines the 2013 Libyan reassessment for 2011, about 9,400 dead, with about 16,012 ACLED-reported fatalities for 2014-2020. Grand_high combines the 21,490 public-health estimate for February 2011-February 2012 with the same 16,012 ACLED 2014-2020 total. Per-side civilian numbers should not be summed because incident datasets overlap and use different definitions.

key_atrocities.name: Misrata siege and indiscriminate shelling
key_atrocities.notes: HRW documented at least 16 civilians killed in specific indiscriminate attacks in April 2011; the full siege toll was higher but not cleanly quantified in the cited sources.

key_atrocities.name: Majer NATO airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: UN Commission documented 34 civilians killed and 38 wounded on 8 August 2011.

key_atrocities.name: Sirte detainee executions after Gaddafi capture
key_atrocities.notes: HRW found evidence of apparent execution of dozens of detainees; secondary summaries describe over 60 victims.

key_atrocities.name: Tajoura migrant detention centre airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: New America reports at least 53 civilians killed in the 3 July 2019 strike.

key_atrocities.name: Murzuq airstrike
key_atrocities.notes: Reported death toll was about 42-43; the LNA confirmed an airstrike but denied targeting civilians.

key_atrocities.name: Tarhuna mass graves and al-Kaniyat disappearances
key_atrocities.notes: GNA authorities reported at least 115 unidentified bodies in 26 mass grave sites by mid-November 2020; many more people were reported missing or abducted.

key_atrocities.name: Post-withdrawal mines, IEDs and booby traps in Tripoli area
key_atrocities.notes: OCHA/Libya Herald reported 206 casualties from explosive contamination since May 2020, including 73 killed.

uncertainty_notes: The most important uncertainty is that no single actor has produced a complete casualty registry for Libya from 2011 through 2020. Reported violent-death datasets, official Libyan reassessments, UN/HRW/Amnesty investigations, and Airwars/New America strike databases overlap but do not use the same definitions. Many 2014-2020 combatant deaths cannot be assigned by side from public sources, and mass graves, missing persons, detention deaths, and explosive-remnant deaths mean that the true death toll may exceed event-dataset counts.
